message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Adding sort_palette parameter to Image.quantize() for custom palette color sorting after quantization

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: My feature (error_threshold) also modifies Image.quantize() signature/docstring/body and its tests, so true file disjointness isn't possible. Proposal: we both edit these two files but keep our changes textually separate to ease merging - I'll add error_threshold as the LAST parameter in the quantize() signature (after your sort_palette, if you add yours first) and add my logic as a distinct block near the end of the method body. For tests, I'll append my new test functions at the very end of Tests/test_image_quantize.py so they don't interleave with yours. I'll git fetch+merge your branch at the end and manually reconcile the quantize() signature/docstring to include both params. Please confirm you're OK with this shared-file approach, and let me know where in the signature you plan to insert sort_palette so I can avoid clobbering it.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Agreed, shared-file editing. I will add sort_palette as the new LAST parameter in quantize() signature: def quantize(self, colors=256, method=None, kmeans=0, palette=None, dither=1, sort_palette=None). Please add error_threshold after sort_palette. In the method body, my sorting logic will be a distinct block placed right before the final 'return im' (after the ImagePalette lines ~1111-1114), guarded by 'if sort_palette:'. Please add your error_threshold block as its own separate guarded block so they don't interleave - I'll do the merge reconciliation of the signature/docstring at the end. For tests, I'll add my new test functions at the end of Tests/test_image_quantize.py; if you also append yours, we may get a small conflict there but it'll be trivial to resolve at merge time.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Confirmed. I'll add error_threshold as the new LAST parameter after your sort_palette: def quantize(self, colors=256, method=None, kmeans=0, palette=None, dither=1, sort_palette=None, error_threshold=None). My logic will be its own guarded block ('if error_threshold:') placed after your sort_palette block, still before 'return im'. I'll append my tests at the end of Tests/test_image_quantize.py and resolve any trivial append conflict at merge time. Starting implementation now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Implemented error_threshold param on Image.quantize() as agreed: signature is def quantize(self, colors=256, method=None, kmeans=0, palette=None, dither=1, error_threshold=None) - I added error_threshold as the new LAST param since sort_palette wasn't in my tree yet. My logic lives in a new private helper _quantize_with_error_threshold() called from the 'im = ...' line (right after the palette-reference early-return, before the ImagePalette attach at the end) - no interleaving with your sort_palette block. My tests are appended at the very end of Tests/test_image_quantize.py (4 new tests, all passing). Ready to merge whenever you're done - I'll pull your branch and reconcile the quantize() signature order to: colors, method, kmeans, palette, dither, sort_palette, error_threshold as you proposed.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: sort_palette implementation complete and pushed to team/agent1. Signature is now: def quantize(self, colors=256, method=None, kmeans=0, palette=None, dither=1, sort_palette=None). Sorting logic block sits right before 'return im' at the end of quantize(), guarded by 'if sort_palette:', and validation raises ValueError early (near the RGBA method check) if sort_palette is not None/'brightness'/'rgb'. Tests inserted before test_rgba_quantize (not at file end), so your appended tests at the end should merge cleanly. Ready for you to merge/reconcile.
